assumption_text: Episode sl-ew4-e3 combines the first NFZ and the transition into the early second NFZ because the strongest civilian casualty source is exactly bounded to 20 Jan-7 Mar 2009 and spans that geometry change.
rationale: Source alignment is better than forcing a split that would sever the best numerator from its own denominator context.

assumption_text: Civilian exposure is approximated by linear interpolation between plausible start and end trapped-population snapshots.
rationale: Daily trapped-population series were not recovered, but source snapshots exist at both ends of the episode.

assumption_text: Low civilian-start scenario uses 250,000 trapped civilians; best/high use 330,000, reflecting the gap between aid-agency and AGA/UN-based estimates.
rationale: This captures the central dispute over the true size of the trapped population without using implausibly low government figures.

assumption_text: End-of-window civilian population is bounded between 130,000 and 200,000, with 175,000 as the best estimate.
rationale: 130,000 is directly grounded in the UN RC/HC note; 150,000-200,000 comes from March 2009 humanitarian planning data.

assumption_text: Average exposed combatants are bounded at roughly 52,000 to 70,000 during the 47-day episode, not the whole Sri Lankan military.
rationale: Reuters provides a direct >50,000 soldier snapshot and ~2,000 LTTE fighter estimate; wider bounds allow attached directly operating units and uncertainty.

assumption_text: Combatant deaths for the episode are model-generated from broad-time-window official combatant series rather than directly observed episode totals.
rationale: No clean Jan 20-Mar 7 combatant death register was found; using explicit model labeling is preferable to false precision.

assumption_text: Unknown-status deaths are strictly excluded from the core DR and not proportionally allocated.
rationale: Available mixed totals explicitly fail to distinguish civilians from LTTE cadres; proportional allocation would add unsupported precision.

assumption_text: The 47-day episode is converted to person-months using a 30-day month.
rationale: Consistent with the project's requested person-month exposure convention.

assumption_text: Core civilian death estimates keep victim_side and inflicting_side null because no numerical attacker split was found for the exact episode window.
rationale: Qualitative evidence strongly suggests most civilian casualties were caused by government fire, but the bundle avoids inferring a numeric split unsupported by an episode-specific source.
